lines.english: He crosses over the world-ocean in the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋਗ ਅਗਨਿ ਤਿਸੁ ਜਨ ਨ ਬਿਆਪੈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: sog agan tis jan na biaapai |2|
lines.english: is not afflicted by the fire of sorrow. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਰਨ ਕਮਲ ਜਾ ਕਾ ਮਨੁ ਰਾਪੈ ॥
lines.translit: charan kamal jaa kaa man raapai |
lines.english: One whose mind is imbued with the Lord's lotus feet

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰ ਧਨ ਦੋਖ ਕਿਛੁ ਪਾਪ ਨ ਫੇੜੇ ॥
lines.translit: par dhan dokh kichh paap na ferre |
lines.english: One who does not steal the wealth of others, who does not commit evil deeds or sinful acts
He chants the Name of the Fearless Lord, and is imbued with the Lord's Love. ||3||
- the Messenger of Death does not even approach him. ||4||
O Nanak, in God's Sanctuary, one is saved. ||5||1||55||
God Himself quenches the fires of desire.
Dhanaasaree, Fifth Mehl:
I am satisfied and satiated, eating the food of Truth.
Life, spiritual life, is in the Lord.
With my mind, body and tongue, I meditate on the Naam, the Name of the Lord. ||1||
Spiritual life consists of chanting the Lord's Name in the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy. ||1||Pause||
He is dressed in robes of all sorts,
if he sings the Kirtan of the Lord's Glorious Praises, day and night. ||2||
Meditating on the Lord's Feet, deep within his mind and body,
He rides upon elephants, chariots and horses,
if he sees the Lord's Path within his own heart. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਸੁਖ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਦਾਸਿ ਪਾਇਆ ॥੪॥੨॥੫੬॥
lines.translit: har sukh nidhaan naanak daas paaeaa |4|2|56|
lines.english: slave Nanak has found the Lord, the treasure of peace. ||4||2||56||
Dhanaasaree, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਮੁੰਦੁ ਸਾਗਰੁ ਜਿਨਿ ਖਿਨ ਮਹਿ ਤਾਰਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: samund saagar jin khin meh taaraa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: They carry it across the world-ocean in an instant. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੇ ਚਰਨ ਜੀਅ ਕਾ ਨਿਸਤਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: gur ke charan jeea kaa nisataaraa |
lines.english: The Guru's feet emancipate the soul.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋਈ ਹੋਆ ਕ੍ਰਮ ਰਤੁ ਕੋਈ ਤੀਰਥ ਨਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: koee hoaa kram rat koee teerath naaeaa |
lines.english: Some love rituals, and some bathe at sacred shrines of pilgrimage.
The Lord Master is the Breaker of bonds.
The Lord's slaves meditate on His Name. ||1||
Servant Nanak meditates in remembrance on the Lord, the Inner-knower, the Searcher of hearts. ||2||3||57||
Dhanaasaree, Fifth Mehl:
The lifestyle of Your slave is so pure,

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਾਸ ਤੇਰੇ ਕੀ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਰੀਤਿ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: daas tere kee niramal reet |1| rahaau |
lines.english: That nothing can break his love for You. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਅ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਮਨ ਧਨ ਤੇ ਪਿਆਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: jeea praan man dhan te piaaraa |
lines.english: He is more dear to me than my soul, my breath of life, my mind and my wealth.
The Lord is the Giver, the Restrainer of the ego. ||1||
This alone is Nanak's prayer. ||2||4||58||
I am in love with the Lord's lotus feet.
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:
Why do you go looking for Him in the forest?
Dhanaasaree, Ninth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਰਬ ਨਿਵਾਸੀ ਸਦਾ ਅਲੇਪਾ ਤੋਹੀ ਸੰਗਿ ਸਮਾਈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sarab nivaasee sadaa alepaa tohee sang samaaee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Although he is unattached, he dwells everywhere. He is always with you as your companion. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਹਰਿ ਬਸੇ ਨਿਰੰਤਰਿ ਘਟ ਹੀ ਖੋਜਹੁ ਭਾਈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: taise hee har base nirantar ghatt hee khojahu bhaaee |1|
lines.english: the Lord dwells deep within; search for Him within your own heart, O Siblings of Destiny. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੁਹਪ ਮਧਿ ਜਿਉ ਬਾਸੁ ਬਸਤੁ ਹੈ ਮੁਕਰ ਮਾਹਿ ਜੈਸੇ ਛਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: puhap madh jiau baas basat hai mukar maeh jaise chhaaee |
lines.english: Like the fragrance which remains in the flower, and like the reflection in the mirror,

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਬਿਨੁ ਆਪਾ ਚੀਨੈ ਮਿਟੈ ਨ ਭ੍ਰਮ ਕੀ ਕਾਈ ॥੨॥੧॥
lines.translit: jan naanak bin aapaa cheenai mittai na bhram kee kaaee |2|1|
lines.english: O servant Nanak, without knowing one's own self, the moss of doubt is not removed. ||2||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਹਰਿ ਭੀਤਰਿ ਏਕੋ ਜਾਨਹੁ ਇਹੁ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨੁ ਬਤਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: baahar bheetar eko jaanahu ihu gur giaan bataaee |
lines.english: Outside and inside, know that there is only the One Lord; the Guru has imparted this wisdom to me.
O Holy people, this world is deluded by doubt.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਕਾ ਸਿਮਰਨੁ ਛੋਡਿਆ ਮਾਇਆ ਹਾਥਿ ਬਿਕਾਨਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: raam naam kaa simaran chhoddiaa maaeaa haath bikaanaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: It has forsaken the meditative remembrance of the Lord's Name, and sold itself out to Maya. ||1||Pause||
Dhanaasaree, Ninth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਤ ਪਿਤਾ ਭਾਈ ਸੁਤ ਬਨਿਤਾ ਤਾ ਕੈ ਰਸਿ ਲਪਟਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: maat pitaa bhaaee sut banitaa taa kai ras lapattaanaa |
lines.english: Mother, father, siblings, children and spouse - he is entangled in their love.
